name: Stochastic CCI Ranging Reversal
edge_hypothesis: CCI Chop Fade proved CCI has ranging alpha (PF=1.43 ranging, PF=1.52 transitional on ETH 4h). Stochastic K/D crossover is a proven cross-based timing mechanism with reliable frequency. The current CCI strategy uses raw CCI threshold zones — but threshold-only entries lack precise timing. By combining CCI extreme zones (< -100 or > 100) as the ZONE CONFIRMATION that price is genuinely oversold/overbought, with Stochastic K crossing D as the TIMING TRIGGER for the actual reversal moment, we get two independent mean-reversion signals agreeing. CHOP > 50 confirms the market is range-bound (the regime where mean-reversion consistently profits). This is a zone+cross hybrid — the zone says WHERE to trade, the cross says WHEN to enter. Note: stochastic_reversal template has an asymmetric k_prev/k_now bug (line 174) — using spec_rules bypasses this entirely with correct crossover logic.

variants.name: stoch_cci_rv_v1_8to1
variants.description: Core thesis: CCI extreme zone + Stochastic K/D crossover + CHOP range gate. 8:1 R:R. CCI < -100 confirms deep oversold, STOCHk crossing above STOCHd times the reversal. CHOP > 50 ensures we only trade in ranges. Pure mean-reversion with double oscillator confirmation.

components.notes: CCI extreme zone detector. < -100 = deeply oversold, > 100 = deeply overbought. Proven ranging alpha (PF=1.43 ranging in CCI Chop Fade).
Stochastic %K — cross-based timing signal. K crossing D within CCI extreme zone times the reversal moment.
Stochastic %D — signal line for K/D crossover detection.
Choppiness Index range gate. > 50 confirms ranging market where mean-reversion profits.
ATR-based position sizing and R:R framework.

variants.name: stoch_cci_rv_v2_ema200_8to1
variants.description: Adds EMA_200 macro trend filter. Tests whether aligning mean-reversion with macro trend direction (only long above EMA_200, short below) improves PF or destroys edge. WARNING: ADX-as-filter killed CCI mean-reversion edge (Entry 005). EMA_200 is different — it's not trend strength but trend direction, which may coexist with ranging conditions. If PF drops, confirms that mean-reversion works best without ANY trend filter.

components.notes: Macro trend direction filter. Tests if trend alignment helps or hurts mean-reversion.

variants.name: stoch_cci_rv_v3_10to1
variants.description: Core thesis at extended 10:1 R:R (15x ATR TP). Tests if CCI-timed mean-reversion can sustain wider TP targets. Mean-reversion typically has shorter holding periods than trend-following — if TP 15 kills PF, confirms 8:1 is the ceiling for this strategy class. If PF holds, suggests these reversals sometimes catch transition moves (ranging-to-trending).

components.notes: CCI extreme zone. Tests if mean-reversion entries can sustain 10:1 R:R.
Stochastic crossover timing at extended TP.

indicator_roles.baseline: CHOP_14_1_100 — Choppiness Index as ranging regime gate. > 50 confirms range-bound market.
indicator_roles.confirmation: CCI_20_0.015 + STOCHk_14_3_3/STOCHd_14_3_3 — Dual mean-reversion: CCI zones identify WHEN price is at extremes, Stochastic crossover times the reversal.
indicator_roles.volume_volatility: ATR_14 — position sizing and R:R framework.
